paras.text: Evening walk pe college ground ke paas, {{A}} aur {{B}} slow chal rahe the, revision karte hue.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ek cheez bahut confuse karti hai mujhe — jab sentence ke end me chhota sa sawaal jodte hain, jaise Hindi me 'hai na?' ya 'nahi kya?' — English me use kya kehte hain?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Usko bolte hain Question Tag. Jaise tum bologe 'Tum aa rahe ho, na?' — English me 'You are coming, aren't you?' Ye chhota sa tag confirm karne ke liye lagate hain.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Achha, but rule kya hai? Kabhi 'aren't', kabhi 'don't', kabhi kuch aur — kaise decide karu?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Simple rule yaad rakho — agar sentence positive hai, to tag NEGATIVE hoga. Aur agar sentence negative hai, to tag POSITIVE hoga. Ab modals ke saath dekhte hain — modal wahi rahega, bas 'not' jodna ya hatana hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ek example do na modal ke saath.

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Chalo suno — You can swim, can't you? — dekho, sentence positive hai ('you can swim'), isliye tag negative hai ('can't you'). Modal 'can' wahi raha, bas 'not' jod diya.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Aur agar sentence pehle se hi negative ho, jaise 'wo nahi aayegi'?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Tab tag positive hoga — 'She won't come, will she?' Dekho, 'won't' me already 'not' hai, isliye sentence negative hai, aur tag me 'not' nahi lagaya, sirf 'will she?'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ohh, matlab jab main confuse hoon ki tag kya lagega, bas ye dekhna hai sentence me 'not' hai ya nahi, aur uska ulta karna hai?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Bilkul sahi! Chalo aur practice karte hain — 'She must leave now' — iska tag kya hoga?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Sentence positive hai ('must leave'), to tag negative chahiye — 'mustn't she?'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Perfect! Ek aur try karo — 'They shouldn't worry about it.'

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ye to already negative hai ('shouldn't'), to tag positive hoga — 'should they?'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi! Dekha, ek baar rule samajh aaya to bahut easy hai. Ab ek tricky wala sunao — Hindi me bologe 'main sahi hoon na?' — isko English me kaise banaoge?

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Hmm, 'I am right' — to tag 'am I not?' hoga?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Yahi sabse bada trap hai SSC me! Sunne me 'am I not' sahi lagta hai, but kabhi bhi normal baat-cheet me aisa nahi bolte, bahut ajeeb/formal lagta hai. Iske liye ek special exception hai — 'I am' ka tag hamesha hota hai I am right, aren't I? — 'I am right, aren't I?' Ye ek exception hai, ratt lo bas.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (notebook me likhte hue) Achha, matlab 'I am' ke saath hamesha 'aren't I' — koi aur logic nahi lagana, bas yaad rakhna. Aur ek doubt — agar sentence me koi modal na ho, sirf normal verb ho, jaise 'You like coffee' — tab tag kaise banega?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Achha sawaal! Jab koi modal na ho, tab helping verb 'do/does/did' use hota hai tag me. Jaise — 'You like coffee, don't you?' Yahan koi modal nahi tha, isliye 'do' use kiya negative tag ke liye.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Ohh, matlab agar modal already hai sentence me (can, will, must, should, etc), to wahi modal tag me bhi jaayega. Aur agar modal nahi hai, to do/does/did use hoga based on tense?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Ekdum sahi! Chalo ek last practice — 'He has finished his homework' — iska tag kya hoga? Dhyan rakhna, ye 'has' modal nahi hai, but helping verb hai.

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: Hmm, 'has' khud hi helping verb hai to... 'hasn't he?'

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: (taali maarte hue) Bilkul perfect! Jab helping verb already sentence me ho (has/have/is/are/was/were), wahi use ho jaata hai tag me, 'do/does/did' ki zarurat nahi padti. Dekha, tumne to poora pattern samajh liya!

paras.speaker: A
paras.text: (muskurate hue) Sab tumhari wajah se — tum sikhaogi na aise hi aage bhi?

paras.speaker: B
paras.text: Sikhaungi na, waise bolo — 'You'll always remember this, won't you?'

quiz.q: Choose the correct question tag: 'She should apologize, ___?'
quiz.explain: Positive statement (should) pe negative tag aata hai (shouldn't), aur subject 'she' pronoun me convert hota hai.

quiz.q: Choose the correct question tag: 'I am your best friend, ___?'
quiz.explain: 'I am' ke sath special rule hai — tag hamesha 'aren't I' hota hai, 'am I not' formal/odd lagta hai casual context me.

quiz.q: Choose the correct question tag: 'They won't come to the party, ___?'
quiz.explain: Sentence already negative hai ('won't'), isliye tag positive hoga — sirf 'will they?', 'not' nahi jodna.

quiz.q: Choose the correct question tag: 'He has completed the project, ___?'
quiz.explain: Jab helping verb (has/have/is/are) already sentence me ho, wahi tag me use hota hai, alag se do/does/did nahi lagate.

quiz.q: Choose the correct question tag: 'You like ice cream, ___?' (no modal or helping verb in the sentence)
quiz.explain: Jab koi modal ya helping verb sentence me na ho, tab tense ke hisab se do/does/did use hota hai tag banane ke liye.

quiz.q: Correct the error: 'She must leave now, mustn't he?'
quiz.answer: She must leave now, mustn't she?
quiz.explain: Tag ka subject sentence ke subject se match karna chahiye — 'she' ke liye 'she' hi aayega tag me, 'he' nahi.

quiz.q: Choose the correct question tag: 'We shall win this match, ___?'
quiz.explain: 'Shall' ke saath formal tag 'shan't we' hota hai, though roz ki baat-cheet me 'won't we' bhi chalta hai.
